zh.lifeSummary: Isabel Ricard是Philip Ricard的女兒，於大約1420年出生在英國布里斯托。她於1442年與Philip Mede在英國布里斯托結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1480年之前。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Isabel Ricard是Philip Ricard的女儿，于大约1420年出生在英国布里斯托。她于1442年与Philip Mede在英国布里斯托结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1480年之前。

nameMeanings.name: Ricard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Andre, Armand, Normand, Raoul, Adelard, Adrien, Amedee, Damien, Fernand, Francois, Germaine.
French: variant of   Richard  . Compare   Recor  ,   Record  ,   Recore  , and   Ricord  .
Channel Islands (Guernsey): variant of Rickard , the characteristic Norman pronunciation of Richard.

variants.name: Recor
meanings: Altered form of French   Ricard  . Compare   Recore  .

variants.name: Ricord
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Christophe, Colette, Fernande, Remi.
Altered form of French Ricard , reflecting the characteristic Canadian and American French rounding of the -ard ending. Compare Record 3.
In some cases possibly also southern French: variant of   Record   4.

variants.name: Richard
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Emile, Jacques, Andre, Marcel, Pierre, Leonce, Normand, Camille, Lucien, Gilles, Henri.

English, French, West Indian (mainly Haiti), German, and Dutch: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements rīc ‘power(ful)’ + hard ‘hardy, brave, strong’. The Middle English forms were Richard and Rikard, respectively Parisian Old French and Old Picard pronunciations of the ancient Germanic name. Middle English Rikard gives rise to the pet form Rick , whilst Richard has given the pet forms Richie and Rich . Both produced rhyming pet forms, Dick and Hick for Rikard and Hitch for Richard.

History: The LA Richard families are mainly descended from Acadian refugees in the second half of the 18th century. — This surname is listed in the register of Huguenot ancestors recognized by the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Recore
meanings: Altered form of French Ricard , reflecting the characteristic Canadian and American French rounding of the -ard ending.

variants.name: Record
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Richeward, Rikeward (Old French Ricoart, ancient Germanic Ricward, from rīc ‘power(ful), realm’ + ward ‘protector’).

English: occasionally perhaps a variant of the Middle English personal name Richold, Rikold, used of both men and women. The male name is Old French, in two different dialect forms, of ancient Germanic Ricoald, Ricold, Richold (from rīc ‘power(ful)’ + wald ‘rule, ruler’), and the female name is an Old French form of ancient Germanic Richold, a variant of Richild (where the second element is hild ‘battle’).
Altered form of French Ricard , reflecting the characteristic Canadian and American French rounding of the -ard ending.

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isobel
meanings: Variant spelling of   Isabel .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: Isabel Ricard was born about 1420, in Bristol, England as the daughter of Philip Ricard. She married Philip Mede in 1442, in Bristol, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. She died before 1480.

fr.lifeSummary: Isabel Ricard est née environ 1420, à Bristol, Angleterre, fille de Philip Ricard. Elle a épousé Philip Mede en 1442, à Bristol, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée avant 1480.

de.lifeSummary: Isabel Ricard wurde um 1420 in Bristol, England, als Tochter von Philip Ricard geboren. Sie hat 1442 in Bristol, England Philip Mede geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb vor 1480.

it.lifeSummary: Isabel Ricard è nata il circa 1420 nel Bristol, England come figlia di Philip Ricard. Lei sposato Philip Mede nel 1442, nel Bristol, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. Morì prima del 1480.

ko.lifeSummary: Isabel Ricard(은)는 대략 1420년 영국 브리스톨에서 Philip Ricard의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1442년 영국 브리스톨에서 Philip Mede(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1480년 이전 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Isabel Ricard родилась около 1420 г. в местности бристол, Англия. Её отца звали Philip Ricard. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Philip Mede в 1442 г. в местности бристол, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла перед 1480 г.
